justification: All pass criteria met: 4 bounded contexts identified (Route Planning, Fleet Management, Delivery Management, Customer Notification). Cross-context dependencies explicitly surfaced in escalations (e.g., multi-driver assignment across Fleet/Route, recalculation triggers spanning Delivery→Route). Vehicle class constraint correctly marked invariant:true (BR-1), stop service time correctly marked invariant:false (BR-7). Route recalculation triggers enumerated in BR-3/4/5. 10 Given/When/Then acceptance criteria provided, well exceeding the 4 minimum.

observations.text: Exceptional depth: 10 business rules with detailed invariant justifications, 10 acceptance criteria, and 10 edge cases far exceed minimum requirements
Vehicle class constraint (BR-1) correctly classified as invariant:true with strong physical/legal justification
Stop service time (BR-7) and notification window (BR-6) correctly classified as invariant:false
Cross-context dependencies explicitly escalated as ambiguities — multi-driver assignment policy, recalculation concurrency, notification behavior across context boundaries
Zone assignment (BR-9) identified as a hard invariant with clear justification — an insight beyond the rubric requirements

Route recalculation triggers (BR-3/4/5) marked as invariant:false. The rubric expected invariant:true since the system must always recalculate on these events. Bird's reasoning (could batch instead) is defensible but diverges from expected classification
Escalations are exceptionally well-structured with concrete options and recommendations, surfacing real integration points for MJ
Edge cases demonstrate deep domain thinking — recalculation storms, GPS signal loss, concurrent triggers, unassignable deliveries
Idempotent notification handling (AC-10) is a sophisticated acceptance criterion that anticipates real production issues
